title: Working with breakpoints
text: Working with breakpoints Set breakpoints on ProcScript instructions, triggers, errors, or conditions. To set or remove a single breakpoint for a ProcScript instruction, you can: Select it in the Module pane of the debugger, right-click and choose Toggle Breakpoint , click the Toggle breakpoint icon ( ), or choose Debug > Toggle Breakpoint . Select it in the Browser window, right-click and choose Toggle Breakpoint , or click the Toggle breakpoint icon ( ). To add, edit, or remove multiple breakpoints multiple breakpoints at specific locations in ProcScript modules: Choose Debug > Edit Breakpoints and click Add . A line is added to the Location list with initial values for Structure and Module names, and for the Details fields. Enter the values for the Details fields, then click OK . Refer to: Managing breakpoints ProcScript Module for breakpoints ProcScript Line for breakpoints Optionally, enter a conditional expression in the Breakpoint conditions field. To remove breakpoints, select the breakpoint in the Location list and click Remove . To set a breakpoint on specific triggers, choose View >Settings and click Triggers . To set a breakpoint on the occurrence of specific ProcScript errors, use the Errors tab of the Settings form. See Working with Error and Exception related breakpoints for details. To set a breakpoint on the occurrence of exceptions, use the Errors tab of the Settings form. See Working with Error and Exception related breakpoints for details. To clear breakpoints, except for breaks on triggers and ProcScript errors, choose Debug > Clear All Breakpoints . Related Links Breakpoints A breakpoint is a marked code line, trigger, error, exception, or condition that forces the application or component to break and the debugger to take control of execution.
